content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

January 13, 1977

A-77-398

The Honorable Raymond L. Sullivan
Associate Justice of the Supreme
Court of California
State Building
San Francisco, California 94102

Dear Justice Sullivan:

Thank you for your letter of January 3, 1977 in which you request advice regarding filing your Statement of Economic Interests.

In your letter you indicate that you will retire as an Associate Justice of the Supreme Court on January 19, 1977. You note the requirements for filing statements pursuant to Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204, and you ask if it is permissible to file one statement.

You may file one statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, and this statement should be filed within 30 days after you leave office. You will not need to file any additional statements.

When you file your statement, you should advise the filing officer of the extended period covered by your statement (January 1, 1977 through January 19, 1977). You may wish to advise the filing officer that your one statement fulfills the filing requirements of Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204.

If you have any questions regarding this procedure, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator

JCG:1h

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

Supreme Court of California
STATE BUILDING
SAN FRANCISCO 94102

RAYMOND L. SULLIVAN
ASSOCIATE JUSTICE

January 3, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California     95804

Attn: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Gentlemen:

     Reference is made to your bulletin dated December 20, 1976, addressed to County Clerks, Clerks of Courts of Record, etc., on the subject of "statements of Economic Interests" required to be filed pursuant to Government Code section 87203. In your bulletin you state that pursuant to regulation adopted by the Commission such statements by judges of courts of record covering the period May 5 through December 31, 1976, must be filed no later than March 1, 1977.

     The undersigned will retire as Associate Justice of the Supreme Court on January 19, 1977. In addition to the annual statement required by section 87203, it would seem that section 87204 requires the filing of a statement of economic interests "within 30 days after leaving the office" and the disclosing of investments and income "during the period since the previous statement filed under sections 87202 or 87203." This presents the anomaly of having the statement filed under section 87204 filed before the annual statement required by section 87203.

     Would you be good enough to advise whether your regulations would permit the filing of one statement covering the period May 5, 1976, through January 19, 1977, and if so, as to the deadline for filing such combined statement. A prompt reply will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

RLS/js                           Raymond L. Sullivan

sections.question: You note the requirements for filing statements pursuant to Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204, and you ask if it is permissable to file one statement.
sections.conclusion: You may file one statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, and this statement should be filed within 30 days after you leave office. You will not need to file any additional statements. When you file your statement, you should advise the filing officer of the extended period covered by your statement (January 1, 1977 through January 19, 1977).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an Associate Justice of the California Supreme Court retiring mid-term may file a single consolidated Statement of Economic Interests covering the partial year period rather than separate statements as required by Government Code Sections 87203 and 87204.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that Justice Sullivan may file one consolidated Statement of Economic Interests covering May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977, to be filed within 30 days of leaving office. No additional statements will be required if the filing officer is notified of the extended period covered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains clear Q&A structure with implicit question and explicit conclusion. Addressed to sitting Supreme Court Justice. References Government Code sections 87203 and 87204. Some OCR artifacts present but content is legible.

When you file your statement, you should advise the filing officer of the extended period covered by your statement (January 1, 1977 through January 19, 1977).

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Justice Raymond L. Sullivan's inquiry regarding the filing of his Statement of Economic Interests upon his retirement from the California Supreme Court on January 19, 1977, advising that a single consolidated statement covering the period May 5, 1976 through January 19, 1977 satisfies all filing requirements.
